channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Colorado two-way star Travis Hunter wins Heisman Trophy as college football's top player
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Travis Hunter turned every play into prime time — on both sides of the ball — and ultimately took home the Heisman Trophy. Now he’s got a leg up on his celebrity coach at Colorado.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: ABC agrees to give $15 million to Donald Trump's presidential library to settle defamation lawsuit
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — ABC News has agreed to pay $15 million toward Donald Trump’s presidential library to settle a defamation lawsuit over anchor George Stephanopoulos' inaccurate on-air assertion that the president-elect had been found civilly liable for raping writer E. Jean Carroll.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Holiday shopping scams to avoid this winter
item.description: Waiting on a plethora of present-related notifications? Don't assume all emails and texts you get are legitimate.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bob Fernandez, a 100-year-old Pearl Harbor survivor, dies peacefully at home 83 years after bombing
item.description: Bob Fernandez, a 100-year-old survivor of the 1941 Japanese bombing of Pearl Harbor, has died peacefully at his nephew's home in Lodi, California.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump nominees should ‘steer clear’ of undermining polio vaccine, McConnell says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Republican leader Mitch McConnell, a childhood polio survivor, said any of President-elect Donald Trump’s nominees seeking confirmation should “steer clear” of efforts to discredit the polio vaccine.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 6 ways to avoid a financial holiday hangover
item.description: The festivity of December is replaced all too quickly by the due dates of January, when the bills from holiday spending and travel arrive.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Storms encase Iowa and eastern Nebraska in ice and generate rare tornado warning in San Francisco
item.description: A major ice storm created treacherous driving conditions across Iowa and eastern Nebraska this weekend and prompted temporary closures of Interstate 80 after numerous cars and trucks slid off the road.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, ABC 4

item.title: Thousands of Santas and a few Grinches hit the streets for annual SantaCon bar crawls
item.description: Thousands of Kris Kringles and jolly Old St. Nicks are jamming streets, sidewalks and bars from Adelaide to Vancouver on Saturday for annual SantaCon charity pub crawls.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A look back at the most notable bankruptcies of 2024
item.description: This year saw its fair share of bankruptcies, with household names and century-old companies in the mix.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KRON4

item.title: Mother of suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing spoke with FBI the night before her son's arrest: report
item.description: The FBI questioned the mother of Luigi Mangione the night before her son's arrest in Pennsylvania, and she said he bore a resemblance to the wanted suspect.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas father set house on fire with 3 children inside: police
item.description: A Texas father is facing charges over allegations that he intentionally set his home on fire with his three children inside, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Winter weather safety: Why bulky coats could be dangerous for children in car seats
item.description: Here are some winter weather tips for drivers, including some on how to make sure children stay safe and warm while in their car seats.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Woman who falsely accused Duke lacrosse players of rape in 2006 publicly admits she lied
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The woman who in 2006 falsely accused three Duke University lacrosse players of raping her — making national headlines that stirred tensions about race, class and the privilege of college athletes — has admitted publicly for the first time that she made up the story.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California DMV apologizes for Cybertruck's offensive license plate
item.description: On Thursday, the license plate in question, which was on a Tesla Cybertruck, was spotlighted in a post to X by StopAntisemitism, a group “dedicated to fighting anti-Semitism.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KRON4

item.title: Biden's commutation in 'kids for cash' scandal angers some Pennsylvania families
item.description: A judge who helped orchestrate one of the worst judicial scandals in U.S.history — a scheme to send children to for-profit jails in exchange for kickbacks — was among the 1,500 people whose sentences were commuted by President Joe Biden this week.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL RUBINKAM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Drones, planes or UFOs? Americans abuzz over mysterious New Jersey sightings
item.description: It’s unclear if that buzzing sound coming out of New Jersey is drones or something else, but for sure the nighttime sightings are producing tons of talk, a raft of conspiracy theories and craned necks looking skyward.
item.dc:creator: Mike Catalini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Prosecutors will seek the death penalty for 2 Venezuelan men accused of killing Texas girl
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Prosecutors in Texas announced Friday that they will seek the death penalty against two Venezuelan men who are accused of killing a 12-year-old Houston girl after they had entered the U.S. illegally.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Conan O'Brien's parents die within days of each other
item.description: Dr. Thomas O’Brien and Ruth R. O’Brien, the parents of late-night icon Conan O’Brien, both passed away within days of each other at their home in Brookline, Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden commutes roughly 1,500 sentences and pardons 39 people in biggest single-day act of clemency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is commuting the sentences of roughly 1,500 people who were released from prison and placed on home confinement during the coronavirus pandemic and is pardoning 39 Americans convicted of nonviolent crimes.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 things to know about Biden’s 1,500 clemencies, pardons
item.description: President Biden pardoned 39 people and commuted the sentences of nearly 1,500 others on Thursday, a sweeping move that comes after pressure from criminal justice advocates over the surprise pardon of his son Hunter Biden.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Amazon to donate $1 million to Trump inauguration, matching Meta
item.description: E-commerce giant Amazon is planning to donate $1 million to President-elect Donald Trump’s inaugural fund, matching the amount Meta already donated to the incoming president’s inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Dick Vitale says he's cancer-free after 4 bouts with disease in just over 3 years
item.description: Vitale had surgery in the summer to remove cancerous lymph nodes from his neck. He was previously treated for melanoma and lymphoma, and had radiation treatments last year for vocal cord cancer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Nearly half of US teens are online 'constantly': Pew report
item.description: Nearly half of American teenagers say they are online “constantly” despite concerns about the effects of social media and smartphones on their mental health, according to a new report published Thursday by the Pew Research Center.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bone-chilling cold returns to the Midwest in the wake of a storm that soaked the East Coast
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Frigid weather returned to the Upper Midwest on Thursday after a storm that swept up the East Coast delivered a blow to New England, packing powerful gusts that knocked out power along with a deluge of rain and warming temperatures that washed away snow and dampened ski resort
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Insurance company denies covering cost of life-saving medicine for medical student
item.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City denied covering the cost of a metro woman’s medicine that could save her life.
item.dc:creator: Malik Jackson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Companies tighten security after a health care CEO's killing leads to a surge of threats
item.description: “Wanted” posters with the names and faces of health care executives have been popping up on the streets of New York. Hit lists with images of bullets are circulating online with warnings that industry leaders should be afraid.
item.dc:creator: DEVNA BOSE and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bill Clinton says he’s open to talking to Biden about preemptive pardon for Hillary Clinton
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton said he is open to having a conversation with President Joe Biden about a potential preemptive pardon for his wife, former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump again named Time's Person of the Year
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump rang the opening bell Thursday at the New York Stock Exchange after being recognized for the second time by Time magazine as its Person of the Year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 2 men go free after their convictions were overturned in a case involving a discredited detective
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Kan. (AP) — Two men were freed Wednesday after their convictions were overturned in a 2009 double homicide whose investigation was overseen by a discredited white Kansas City, Kansas, police detective.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Atmospheric river brings weather whiplash to East Coast as bomb cyclone develops
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — The U.S. East Coast began a whiplash-inducing stretch of weather Wednesday with a deluge of rain, rapid snowmelt and powerful gusts, creating dangerous conditions, due in part to an atmospheric river and developing bomb cyclone.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, ABC 4
